models.model_summary: Compact sedan available with a 150-hp 2.0L gas engine (LX, Sport) or a 200-hp two-motor hybrid powertrain (Sport Hybrid, Sport Touring Hybrid). Front-wheel drive only. Carries top crash ratings from IIHS and NHTSA. Honda Sensing ADAS suite is standard across all trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026, measuring 2023 MY vehicles) did not list the Civic among model-level award winners but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. Consumer Reports' 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey rates Honda overall at 59 (#4 brand), and CR named the 2026 Civic as a Top Pick (Best Small Car) citing 'strong predicted reliability' from carryover 11th-gen architecture. The 11th-gen platform (introduced 2022) has rated above average across MY 2022-2025.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Carryover 11th-gen Civic scored at or above mass-market average in 2024-2025 APEAL. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to praise the 11th-gen Civic Sedan for its refined ride, intuitive cabin layout, and the new hybrid trims for delivering near-sport-sedan acceleration (0-60 in ~6.4 sec) alongside ~49 mpg combined. The 150-hp gas powertrain in LX/Sport is considered adequate but unexciting; reviewers consistently recommend stepping up to the hybrid where budget allows.

owner_reviews.summary: Too early in the 2026 MY cycle for meaningful owner-review samples on the 2026 Civic Sedan specifically; the carryover 11th-gen platform has consistently averaged ~4.5 stars on Edmunds for prior MYs with praise for fuel economy and complaints about road noise.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 215/55R16 all-season on steel wheels with covers

trims.notes: EPA annual fuel cost not directly published for this exact trim; left null. Destination fee shown is the Honda-published $1,195 for 2026 Civic; some dealer pages quote $1,150 — the manufacturer source could not be opened directly due to 403.

notable_other: Smart Entry with Push Button Start

Smart Entry with Push Button Start and Remote Engine Start (LX has key entry)

Same 2.0L NA engine, CVT, FWD as LX
Two-motor hybrid powertrain: 200 hp combined system output vs 150 hp gas (+50 hp)
0-60 mph drops to ~6.4 sec vs 9.8 sec
9-inch infotainment with wireless CarPlay/AA replaces 7-inch wired
Standard one-touch power moonroof, heated front seats
Curb weight rises to 3,208 lb vs 2,875 lb due to battery/motor

trims.notes: Hybrid battery is a small lithium-ion pack (~1.05 kWh) — not user-chargeable, so DC/AC fast-charge fields are null. Hybrid powertrain system net output is 200 hp; engine alone produces ~141 hp per Honda press materials.

notable_other: Google Built-in with Google Assistant and Google Maps

changes: Two-motor hybrid powertrain (200 hp) and ~49 mpg combined (same as Sport Hybrid)
Leather-trimmed seats with red stitching replace cloth
Bose 12-speaker Premium Sound System with Centerpoint surround
Google Built-in and Alexa Built-in added

trims.notes: Powertrain/fuel-economy/dimensions identical to Sport Hybrid trim — set to null per spec rule (do not copy values from base or peer trims).

models.notes: All four Civic Sedan trims share the same image set within their respective trim families (gas vs hybrid). MPG figures listed are EPA-published values; manufacturer pages cite same figures. Destination fee $1,195 confirmed via Honda press release search; $1,150 also seen on some secondary sources — verified as $1,195 from hondanews summary. Civic Si is treated as a separate model (own entry below).

models.generation_context: 11th-gen, introduced 2022; refreshed for 2025-26 MY. Built in Indiana.
models.model_summary: Hatchback variant of the Civic, sharing the sedan's wheelbase but with a shorter overall length and a liftback opening to 24.5 cu ft of cargo. No LX trim — Sport is the base. Same 2.0L NA gas and 200-hp two-motor hybrid powertrains as the sedan, both FWD. IIHS rates the hatch a TOP SAFETY PICK (vs the sedan's TSP+) due to a different headlight rating.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not list the Civic Hatchback among model-level award winners; Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. Consumer Reports 2026 brand reliability score: Honda 59 (#4). The Hatchback shares its 11th-gen platform and powertrains with the Civic sedan, which CR named a 2026 Top Pick (Best Small Car) for strong predicted reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Carryover 11th-gen Civic platform scored at or above mass-market average in 2024-2025 APEAL. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers favor the hatchback over the sedan for its added cargo flexibility and slightly more upscale stance, while powertrains and driving manners are identical. Sport Touring Hybrid is widely considered the value pick of the lineup, delivering 200 hp and ~48 mpg combined for under $34k MSRP.

owner_reviews.summary: Too early in the 2026 MY for owner samples; prior-MY hatchbacks averaged ~4.5 stars with consistent praise for cargo utility and complaints about cabin road noise.

trims.notes: Civic Hatchback Sport ships with one-touch power moonroof standard — unlike the sedan Sport. 0-60 listed as estimated; manufacturer does not publish for this exact trim, but motormatchup quotes ~9.5 sec for similar gas Civic configurations.

changes: Two-motor hybrid powertrain: 200 hp / 232 lb-ft system output vs 150 hp / 133 lb-ft (+50 hp, +99 lb-ft)
EPA combined MPG rises to 48 vs 34 (+14)
0-60 mph drops to ~6.4 sec vs ~9.5 sec
Synthetic leather seats replace cloth; heated front seats and heated steering wheel added
10.2-inch digital instrument cluster vs 7-inch analog/info cluster
Curb weight rises ~284 lb to 3,260 lb due to hybrid components

trims.notes: Hybrid uses small lithium-ion pack (~1.05 kWh) — not user-chargeable, so DC/AC fields null. Hatchback hybrid MPG (50/45/48) is slightly lower than sedan hybrid (50/47/49) due to aero/weight differences.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 235/40R18 on exclusive 18-inch alloy wheels with Matte Shark Gray inserts

features.sound_system: Bose 12-speaker premium audio with Centerpoint surround
notable_other: Google Built-in with Google Assistant and Google Maps

changes: Two-motor hybrid powertrain (200 hp / 48 mpg combined) — same as Sport Hybrid
Leather-trimmed seats with 8-way power driver seat vs cloth manual
9-inch touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/AA + Google Built-in vs 7-inch wired
Bose 12-speaker premium sound system with Centerpoint
Front and rear parking sensors, rain-sensing wipers, Low-Speed Braking Control

trims.notes: Powertrain/fuel-economy/dimensions identical to Sport Hybrid — set null per spec rule.

models.notes: Manufacturer's consumer-site images shared across trim families (gas vs hybrid). IIHS rates the Civic Hatchback TSP (not TSP+) — the sedan earns TSP+ — due to a difference in available headlights. Curb weights for hybrid hatchback are estimated based on sedan hybrid + typical hatch body penalty; manufacturer figures vary slightly by source.

models.generation_context: 11th-gen Civic Si, introduced for 2022 MY; treated as its own model line by Honda (separate model page) — sedan-based four-door performance variant
models.model_summary: Single-trim performance Civic sedan with a 200-hp turbocharged 1.5L engine, six-speed manual transmission (no automatic offered), and a helical limited-slip differential. Sits between the regular Civic Sport and the Type R in the performance hierarchy. Aimed at enthusiasts who want a daily-driver-friendly entry sport sedan.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not list the Civic Si specifically; Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR 2026 Honda brand reliability score: 59 (#4). The Civic Si shares the 11th-gen platform with the standard Civic that CR named a 2026 Top Pick for strong predicted reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released. The Si is low-volume relative to the standard Civic and may not receive a separate APEAL ranking.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to single out the Si as one of the best-handling sub-$35k sedans, delivering manual-transmission engagement, a free-revving 1.5L turbo, and Type-R-derived chassis hardware in a more affordable, easier-to-live-with package than the Type R. The downside is acceleration that's slower than a Civic Hybrid (0-60 ~6.6 vs 6.4 sec).

owner_reviews.summary: Too few 2026 MY owner reviews to summarize; prior MY Civic Si averaged ~4.5 stars on Edmunds with praise for steering feel and shifter and complaints about firm ride and limited cargo room vs hatchback.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 235/40R18 summer-capable performance tires on 18-inch matte-black alloy wheels

notable_other: performance sport seats with red stitching
exclusive Si exterior styling and badging

trims.notes: Single trim — base by default. Premium fuel required per Honda spec for the turbocharged engine. Ground clearance ~4.3 in is approximate; manufacturer publishes lower than standard Civic due to sport-tuned suspension. 14.4 cu ft trunk vs LX 14.8 reflects sport-seat package.

models.notes: Honda markets the Si on its own model page (automobiles.honda.com/civic-si-sedan) — treated as a separate model per spec rules. Pricing per Edmunds and Honda press; the $31,495 figure represents starting MSRP as published; destination is listed separately at $1,195.

models.generation_context: FL5 / 11th-gen Type R, introduced for 2023 MY; flagship Honda performance car for the US market
models.model_summary: Single-trim hot hatch with a 315-hp turbocharged 2.0L K20C1 engine, six-speed manual transmission, helical limited-slip differential, and adaptive dampers. The chassis hardware donates to the new Prelude. FWD only — Honda does not offer an automatic or AWD Type R. Holds the front-wheel-drive Nurburgring lap record for its class.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not list the Civic Type R (FL5) — typical for very low-volume halo models. CR 2026 Honda brand reliability score: 59 (#4). Sample sizes are too small for a model-specific CR predicted reliability score; carryover FL5 platform has had no major reliability issues reported across MY 2023-2025.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The Type R is very low-volume and unlikely to receive a model-specific APEAL score even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: The Type R continues to win editor's-choice and best-handling awards from major outlets, lauded for steering precision, chassis balance, and one of the best manual gearboxes on sale. Critics note the polarizing styling, an obviously commercial 0.8-sec slower 0-60 than a Civic Hybrid is overcome by drive engagement, and dealer markups remain a real-world buyer pain point in the US.

owner_reviews.summary: Sample sizes still small. Prior MY Type R owners give it ~4.7+ stars on Edmunds; common praise: shifter feel and chassis. Common complaints: dealer ADM/markups, narrow seat side bolster for larger drivers.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 265/30ZR19 Michelin Pilot Sport 4S summer tires on 19-inch matte-black alloy wheels

notable_other: high-grip Type R sport seats (red Alcantara/cloth mix)
Type R-specific aero (rear wing, front splitter, side skirts)

trims.notes: $46,895 base MSRP is derived from the widely-reported $48,090 'including destination' figure minus the $1,195 destination fee. Honda press release for 2026 Type R pricing returned 403; primary cite is Cars.com pricing article. Seating capacity is 4 (not 5) — Type R deletes center rear seat in favor of fixed structural cross-brace. IIHS does not separately rate the Type R hatchback variant; NHTSA ratings also not published for this niche performance variant — set to null.

models.notes: Treated as its own model line per spec rules — Honda gives Type R its own model page (automobiles.honda.com/civic-type-r). Premium fuel required. Production volume is limited; dealer ADM is common in the US market but is not part of MSRP.

models.generation_context: 11th-gen Accord, introduced for 2023 MY; sportier styling and additional standard tech for 2026 MY
models.model_summary: Midsize family sedan offered in two gas trims (LX, SE with 192-hp 1.5L turbo) and four hybrid trims (Sport Hybrid, EX-L Hybrid, Sport-L Hybrid, Touring Hybrid — all with 204-hp two-motor hybrid). FWD only. Earns IIHS TOP SAFETY PICK+ and NHTSA 5-star overall.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not award the Accord, but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR's 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey gives Honda a brand reliability score of 59 (#4); the Accord specifically posted a reliability score of 74 — tied with the Toyota Camry for the second-best in the midsize-sedan category. Current 11th-gen Accord has rated above average across MY 2023-2025.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Carryover 11th-gen Accord has scored at or above midsize-sedan APEAL average in 2024-2025. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to call the Accord one of the segment's most polished and rewarding-to-drive midsize sedans. The hybrid trims (especially EX-L) deliver 48 mpg combined — class-leading except for Camry — without sacrificing performance. Critics flag the LX/SE's bare-bones 1.5T as adequate but uninspiring and recommend stepping up to a hybrid.

owner_reviews.summary: Early 2026 owner reviews limited; recent MY Accords average ~4.5 stars on Edmunds with praise for ride comfort and complaints about lack of AWD option in a snowy-climate context.

trims.notes: Fuel tank capacity 14.7 gal verified per Honda press release range; published curb weight 3,239 lb is the base figure — heavier trims vary by ~80-200 lb.

changes: Two-motor hybrid powertrain replaces 1.5T: 204 hp / 247 lb-ft vs 192 hp / 192 lb-ft (+12 hp, +55 lb-ft)
12.3-inch touchscreen with Google Built-in replaces 7-inch wired
Wireless phone charging, heated front seats, 8-way power driver seat
Curb weight rises ~230 lb to 3,468 lb

trims.notes: Hybrid battery ~1.06 kWh, not user-chargeable. Fuel tank smaller than LX (12.8 vs 14.7 gal) — Honda uses a smaller tank in hybrids to retain trunk space.

changes: EPA 51/44/48 — highest MPG in Accord lineup (smaller 17-inch wheels improve efficiency vs Sport Hybrid)

trims.notes: EX-L gets best MPG (48) because it uses smaller 17-inch wheels than the Sport Hybrid's 19-inch — a trade-off Honda highlights in press materials.

changes: EPA 46/41/44 — sport-styled trim takes a small MPG hit vs EX-L due to 19-inch wheels
Leather-trimmed seats with 12-way power driver seat and memory

trims.notes: Combines hybrid powertrain with sport styling vs EX-L's comfort focus.

changes: Hybrid powertrain (204 hp / 247 lb-ft) and 44 mpg combined
Head-up display added (unique to Touring in lineup)
Ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel

trims.notes: Top trim. Only Accord with head-up display. Same powertrain as the other hybrid trims.

models.notes: Lineup is 2 gas trims (LX, SE) + 4 hybrid trims (Sport Hybrid, EX-L Hybrid, Sport-L Hybrid, Touring Hybrid). No AWD option offered. Hybrid pack is non-pluggable (~1.06 kWh) — DC/AC fast-charge fields null. EX-L Hybrid uses 17-inch wheels (vs 19-inch on other hybrids) which improves its EPA to 48 mpg combined.

models.generation_context: All-new 6th-gen Prelude (BF1), revived for 2026 MY after a 24-year hiatus. Built on Civic platform; chassis hardware shared with Civic Type R (adaptive dampers, suspension tuning).
models.model_summary: Two-door hybrid sports coupe with a 200-hp two-motor hybrid powertrain and chassis hardware borrowed from the Civic Type R. Available as a single Hybrid trim with an optional contrasting black-roof Two-Tone for $500 more. FWD only. Honda markets it as a fun-to-drive grand tourer rather than an outright sports car — tested 0-60 in ~6.5 sec is slower than rivals like the Toyota GR86.

reliability.summary: All-new 2026 Prelude has no JD Power 2026 VDS coverage (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles) and no Consumer Reports model-specific predicted reliability score. The hybrid powertrain is shared with the Civic/Accord hybrids (which CR rates well) and the chassis hardware donates from the Civic Type R. Honda brand CR 2026 reliability score: 59 (#4).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The all-new Prelude has no APEAL history; the low-volume halo coupe may not receive a separate ranking even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers find the Prelude polarizing: praised for steering feel, cabin design, and the Type-R-derived chassis, but criticized for acceleration that's slower than its 200-hp twin (Civic Hybrid Hatch) and a price tag closer to a Mustang EcoBoost. Most outlets position it as a refined personal coupe, not a sports car.

owner_reviews.summary: Too early — Prelude just began deliveries; no statistically meaningful owner-review sample yet.

powertrain.transmission: Two-motor hybrid direct drive with S+ Shift simulated-gear paddle shifters

trims.notes: Single mechanical configuration; only 'Hybrid Two-Tone' trim adds a contrasting black roof. NHTSA / IIHS have not yet rated the Prelude — set to null. 0-60 time of 6.5 sec is per Car and Driver testing; ~7.3 sec when S+ Shift simulated-gear paddles are engaged.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 235/40R19 96V on 19x8.5J Berlina Black alloy wheels (same as Hybrid)

changes: Adds contrasting black roof (appearance package only)
All mechanical and feature content identical to Hybrid trim

trims.notes: Pure appearance package — black roof only. Interior images shared with the base trim family.

models.notes: All-new model for 2026 — North American launch is November 2025. Honda treats this as a single-trim car with a single appearance variant (Two-Tone). Production volume is limited; Honda has not announced US allocation totals. The Prelude is mechanically a Civic Hybrid Hatchback under the skin, with Type-R chassis hardware bolted on.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen HR-V, introduced for 2023 MY; mild styling and tech update for 2026 — 9-inch touchscreen now standard on all trims
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover SUV based on the Civic platform. Three trims (LX, Sport, EX-L) all powered by a 158-hp 2.0L naturally aspirated four; FWD standard with optional Real Time AWD. No hybrid powertrain offered (unlike its CR-V sibling). Earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ rating.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not award the HR-V at the model level, but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR 2026 brand reliability score: Honda 59 (#4). HR-V's carryover 3rd-gen platform (built on Civic architecture) has tracked above average for subcompact-SUV reliability across MY 2023-2025.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note that the HR-V's 158-hp engine feels underpowered for highway merging and that fuel economy lags hybrid rivals like the Toyota Corolla Cross Hybrid. On the positive side, the cabin is well-finished for the price and Honda Sensing is standard.

owner_reviews.summary: Early 2026 owner samples small. Recent MY HR-Vs average ~4.3 stars on Edmunds with praise for cabin space and visibility, complaints about acceleration and engine noise.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 215/60R17 all-season on 17-inch Pewter Gray alloy wheels

trims.notes: Real-Time AWD is a $1,500 option on the LX/Sport (-1 MPG combined). Destination fee for HR-V is typically $1,450 (truck/SUV bracket — higher than sedan).

0.3-inch taller height with 0.3-inch extra ground clearance

models.notes: All-wheel drive (Real Time AWD) is a $1,500 option on every trim — adds ~150 lb of curb weight and drops EPA combined MPG by ~1 (to 25 city / 30 hwy / 27 combined). 2026 standardizes the 9-inch touchscreen across all trims, replacing the prior 7-inch on LX/Sport.

models.generation_context: 6th-gen CR-V, introduced for 2023 MY; carryover for 2026 with minor packaging updates
models.model_summary: Compact two-row crossover SUV — Honda's best-selling vehicle in the US. Three gas trims (LX, EX, EX-L) with 190-hp 1.5L turbo, and four hybrid trims (Sport, TrailSport, Sport-L, Sport Touring) with 204-hp two-motor hybrid. AWD optional on most trims; standard on TrailSport Hybrid and Sport Touring Hybrid. Towing 1,500 lb (gas) or 1,000 lb (hybrid).

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not award the CR-V at the model level, but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR 2026 brand reliability score: Honda 59 (#4); CR specifically noted that the CR-V Hybrid 'scored near the top of its respective category' in the 2026 reliability survey. Current 6th-gen CR-V has rated above average across MY 2023-2025; the hybrid powertrain is shared with Accord Hybrid.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the CR-V near the top of the compact-SUV segment for its spacious interior, smooth ride, and refined hybrid powertrain. The TrailSport Hybrid trim is praised for adding light off-road credibility (all-terrain tires, increased ground clearance) while the Sport Touring Hybrid is recommended as the segment's best balance of luxury features and fuel economy.

owner_reviews.summary: Early 2026 owner samples small. Carryover 6th-gen CR-V averages ~4.5 stars on Edmunds with broad praise for utility/space and frequent complaints about base 1.5T noise under acceleration.

trims.notes: Real-Time AWD available for $1,500 (-1 MPG combined). 0-60 ~8.5 sec is per Car and Driver historic testing of 1.5T CR-V — manufacturer does not publish.

changes: 9-inch touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/AA replaces 7-inch wired
Two-motor hybrid powertrain replaces 1.5T: 204 hp / 247 lb-ft vs 190 / 179 (+14 hp, +68 lb-ft)
Heated front seats, 8-way power driver seat, wireless charging
Towing capacity drops to 1,000 lb vs 1,500 lb

trims.notes: Real-Time AWD optional ($1,500). AWD reduces EPA to 40/34/37 combined.

changes: Same gloss-black 18-inch wheels as Sport Hybrid

trims.notes: Top CR-V trim. Only CR-V with head-up display. 19-inch wheels reduce MPG slightly vs Sport-L (37 vs 40 combined AWD).

models.notes: Three gas trims (LX/EX/EX-L with 1.5L turbo) and four hybrid trims (Sport/TrailSport/Sport-L/Sport Touring with 204-hp 2.0L hybrid). Real-Time AWD is optional on LX, EX, EX-L, Sport Hybrid, and Sport-L Hybrid; standard on TrailSport Hybrid and Sport Touring Hybrid. Hybrid pack ~1.06 kWh non-pluggable. Honda also offers a CR-V e:FCEV hydrogen variant in CA only — excluded from this catalog because availability is geographically limited and the fuel infrastructure is not a national offering.

models.generation_context: All-new 3rd-gen Passport (YF11), introduced for 2026 MY. Body-on-frame-influenced architecture shared with global Pilot platform; styling repositions Passport as a more rugged off-road-capable two-row SUV.
models.model_summary: All-new two-row midsize SUV repositioned as Honda's off-road-capable model. 3.5L V6 with 285 hp and 10-speed automatic, AWD (i-VTM4) standard across all trims. Seven trims organized in two groups: RTL (on-road) and TrailSport (off-road). 5,000-lb tow rating, 8.3 in ground clearance, all-terrain tires standard on TrailSport. Earned IIHS TOP SAFETY PICK+ rating.

reliability.summary: All-new 3rd-gen Passport for 2026 — no JD Power 2026 VDS coverage (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles) and no Consumer Reports model-specific predicted reliability. Honda's brand-level CR 2026 reliability score is 59 (#4). The Passport shares its V6 and 10-speed automatic with the redesigned Pilot.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The all-new Passport has no APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Passport for genuinely improved off-road capability (standard all-terrain tires on TrailSport, increased ground clearance, skid plates) while retaining a comfortable on-road ride. The V6 is unchanged and fuel economy lags hybrid rivals like the Toyota 4Runner and Kia Sorento Hybrid. Honda Sensing 360+ is standard.

owner_reviews.summary: Brand-new model — minimal owner reviews. Outgoing 2nd-gen Passport averaged ~4.5 stars on Edmunds with praise for V6 power and complaints about MPG.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 265/60R18 all-season highway tires on 18-inch alloy wheels

trims.notes: Destination $1,495 for Passport (truck/SUV bracket). i-VTM4 AWD is standard across all Passport trims — no FWD option.

trims.notes: Top trim with blackout appearance package. $53,850 base is approximate — dealer-published MSRP rounded from press materials.

models.notes: All-new model for 2026. All trims standard with i-VTM4 AWD; no FWD option. Seven trims organized in three families: RTL (on-road), TrailSport (off-road), and TrailSport Elite (off-road with luxury). 'Blackout' packages are appearance-only and add $1,200 over their respective base trim. No hybrid powertrain offered. Earned IIHS TOP SAFETY PICK+ rating for 2026 — Honda's most rugged SUV but still meets top safety standards.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Pilot, introduced for 2023 MY; mild styling and tech refresh for 2026 with more standard tech
models.model_summary: Three-row family SUV with 3.5L V6 (285 hp), 10-speed automatic, and FWD standard / i-VTM4 AWD optional or standard (depending on trim). Seven trims: Sport, EX-L, TrailSport, Touring, Touring Blackout, Elite, Black Edition. Seats 7 (TrailSport with captain's chairs) or 8 (others with stowable 2nd-row center seat). Up to 5,000-lb towing AWD.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not award the Pilot at the model level. Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability (CR 2026 brand reliability: 59, #4). Current 4th-gen Pilot has trailed segment leaders slightly on JD Power VDS for MY 2023-2024 but is on par with Honda brand averages; CR predicted reliability has trended average-to-above-average.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note that the Pilot offers more usable third-row space than most rivals, a comfortable highway ride, and (in TrailSport guise) real off-road capability. The unhybridized V6 is the main critique — fuel economy lags Toyota Grand Highlander Hybrid and Kia Telluride significantly.

owner_reviews.summary: Early 2026 sample limited; current-gen Pilot averages ~4.4 stars on Edmunds with praise for space and family-friendliness; complaints center on fuel economy and 10-speed transmission shift smoothness in city driving.

trims.notes: AWD optional on Sport (+$2,000 typical). FWD towing is 3,500 lb; AWD towing rises to 5,000 lb with trailer prep. Dimensions estimated from carsfrenzy.net; some sources quote 194.5-199.9 in length depending on package — using 199.9 in for current refresh.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 265/60R18 all-season on 18-inch alloy wheels (different design vs Sport)

changes: 12-way power driver seat with memory (Sport is 8-way no memory)

trims.notes: AWD optional. Same powertrain and dimensions as Sport.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 265/60R18 General Grabber A/TX all-terrain tires on 18-inch matte-black alloy wheels

Heated steering wheel and leather-trimmed seats
Towing rises to 5,000 lb (with trailer prep)
EPA drops to 18/23/20 due to AWD + A/T tires

trims.notes: Off-road-focused trim with captain's chairs. Replaces 8-seat configuration with 7-seat for second-row comfort.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 265/50R20 all-season on 20-inch Shark Gray alloy wheels

changes: AWD typically standard at this trim (verify in build-and-price)
Ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel
Blackout exterior package: black wheels, blackout badging, gloss-black trim
Mechanically and feature-wise identical to Touring

trims.notes: Pure appearance package.

changes: Panoramic glass roof replaces standard sunroof
Nappa leather seats replace standard leather
2nd-row captain's chairs (7 seats vs 8 on Touring)

trims.notes: Top luxury trim with captain's chairs and panoramic roof.

changes: Black Edition appearance package: exclusive black 20-inch wheels, blackout badging
Black interior with red ambient lighting accents
Mechanically and feature-wise identical to Elite

trims.notes: Top-of-line. Black Edition appearance package over Elite.

models.notes: Seven trims. AWD optional on Sport/EX-L/Touring (and is the typical configuration). AWD standard on TrailSport, Elite, Black Edition. 2nd-row captain's chairs (7 seats total) on TrailSport, Elite, Black Edition; stowable 8-seat configuration on Sport/EX-L/Touring. Dimensions and curb weight ranges vary slightly between FWD and AWD.

models.generation_context: 2nd MY of Honda's first US-market BEV; co-developed with GM on the Ultium platform (shares architecture with Chevy Blazer EV / Acura ZDX). For 2026 Honda gained native NACS-CCS adapter and various tech updates.
models.model_summary: Two-row all-electric midsize SUV with 85-kWh battery, 220-hp single-motor FWD (EX/Touring) or 300-hp dual-motor AWD (EX/Touring optional, Elite standard). Up to 308 miles of EPA range. DC fast charge at 155 kW (20-80% in ~35 min). Honda's first US electric vehicle, replacing the Clarity EV.

reliability.summary: Insufficient data — 2025 MY Prologue was the first MY and had some software/recall issues; CR predicted reliability has been at the lower end of Honda's range. No 2026 VDS yet.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The Prologue is only in its 2nd MY of US sales and may not yet meet APEAL's sample-size threshold. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Prologue for its spacious cabin, comfortable ride, and competitive range/charging speed. The dual-motor AWD versions are praised for responsive acceleration. Critics flag the GM-derived underpinnings (some interface awkwardness) and a price/range ratio that's no longer class-leading versus 2026 EV rivals like the Hyundai Ioniq 5 and Kia EV9.

owner_reviews.summary: Early 2025 Prologue owner reviews on Edmunds averaged ~4.3 stars with praise for ride comfort and charging speed; complaints centered on early-MY software glitches and a sparse one-pedal driving system.

notable_other: heated front seats and heated steering wheel
NACS-CCS adapter included for Tesla Supercharger access

trims.notes: EV battery warranty per federal CARB-compliant standard 8yr/100k. AC onboard charger 11.5 kW (Level 2) estimated based on GM Ultium platform — Honda has not publicly broken this out for EX trim. Curb weight estimated from Chevy Blazer EV FWD (~5,300 lb) — Honda does not separately publish.

changes: Leather-trimmed seats with memory replace synthetic leather

trims.notes: 21-inch wheels may reduce range slightly vs EX's 19-inch — manufacturer publishes the same 308 mi figure but real-world differs. AWD optional ($3,000), reduces range to 294 mi.

changes: Dual-motor AWD standard (300 hp / 355 lb-ft vs 220 / 243 single-motor FWD)
EPA range drops to 283 mi (vs 308 FWD EX) due to AWD + 21-inch wheels

trims.notes: Top trim with AWD standard. 1,500-lb tow rating same across all trims.

models.notes: All-electric model on GM Ultium platform. EX and Touring offered as single-motor FWD or dual-motor AWD ($3,000 option, reduces range from 308 to 294 mi). Elite is AWD-only. NACS-CCS adapter included from 2026 onward, enabling Tesla Supercharger access. Curb weights estimated from related Chevy Blazer EV platform — Honda does not publicly publish curb weight figures by trim.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Ridgeline (YK2), refreshed for 2024; 2026 carries over with minor color/option updates
models.model_summary: Honda's only pickup truck — a unibody midsize crew-cab with 3.5L V6 (280 hp), 9-speed automatic, and i-VTM4 AWD standard. Single 5.3-ft bed length. Five-trim lineup: Sport (base), RTL (luxury), TrailSport (off-road), TrailSport+HPD Wheels (appearance), Black Edition (top luxury). Up to 5,000 lb towing and 1,583 lb payload. Unique among midsize trucks for its in-bed trunk and ride comfort.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) named the Toyota Tacoma as the midsize-pickup award winner; the Ridgeline was not awarded but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR 2026 Honda brand reliability score: 59 (#4). Current-gen Ridgeline has trailed Tacoma slightly in JD Power VDS but typically rates above midsize-truck average and benefits from a low-stress unibody powertrain.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Ridgeline the most car-like midsize truck — ride and handling are excellent for the class, and the in-bed trunk + lockable trunk are unique selling points. Critics note that 5,000 lb tow is lowest in segment and that the unibody construction can't match body-on-frame Tacoma/Ranger for serious off-road work.

owner_reviews.summary: Current-gen Ridgeline averages ~4.7 stars on Edmunds with very high owner satisfaction; complaints are mostly about fuel economy and the lack of hybrid option.

trims.notes: All Ridgeline trims are AWD only — no FWD option. Single 64-inch crew-cab bed configuration; no extended cab or short/long bed options. IIHS has not awarded TSP/TSP+ to the current Ridgeline — set to null. Schema extension: added bed_length_in and bed_volume_cuft inside cargo_volume_cuft per spec §truck handling.

changes: Leather-trimmed seats with memory replace cloth

EPA drops 1 mpg combined (20 vs 21) due to A/T tires

Black exterior trim, badging, and black alloy wheels

trims.notes: Top trim with luxury features. Two-Tone variant adds $500 for contrasting black roof.

models.notes: Single cab/bed configuration (crew cab, 5.3-ft bed). i-VTM4 AWD is standard on all trims — no FWD option. The Ridgeline's unibody construction is unique in midsize-truck segment (vs body-on-frame Tacoma/Ranger/Frontier). Cargo schema extended with bed_length_in and bed_volume_cuft fields per truck handling rule in §spec. Touring trim from prior MY was discontinued; 2026 lineup is the five trims above. Black Edition Two-Tone color variant adds $500 to MSRP but is treated as a color option, not a separate trim.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Odyssey (RL6); mid-cycle refresh for 2026 MY with updated front fascia, lighting, and additional standard tech
models.model_summary: 8-seat minivan with 3.5L V6 (280 hp), 10-speed automatic, FWD only — Honda does not offer AWD or a hybrid Odyssey. Four-trim lineup (EX-L, Sport-L, Touring, Elite). Magic Slide 2nd-row seats, leather standard on all trims, CabinTalk standard. Top Touring/Elite trims add CabinWatch rear-seat camera and rear-seat entertainment.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) named the Toyota Sienna as the minivan segment award winner; the Odyssey was not awarded but Honda as a brand sits in the upper-mid tier of mass-market dependability. CR 2026 Honda brand reliability score: 59 (#4). Current 5th-gen Odyssey has shown average-to-above-average reliability on JD Power for MY 2023-2025.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to call the Odyssey a top-segment minivan for family use, praising the Magic Slide seat system, smooth ride, and well-finished cabin. Critics note that the absence of a hybrid powertrain (unlike Chrysler Pacifica Hybrid or Toyota Sienna Hybrid) leaves it behind on fuel economy.

owner_reviews.summary: Current-gen Odyssey averages ~4.4 stars on Edmunds with broad praise for family functionality and complaints about fuel economy (vs hybrid rivals) and lack of AWD.

notable_other: Magic Slide 2nd-row seats with Easy Tilt

trims.notes: Leather seats are standard on every Odyssey trim — EX-L is the base. IIHS does not currently rate the Odyssey at the TSP/TSP+ level — set to null. Trunk cuft (behind 3rd row) ~38.6 cu ft. Total 155.8 cu ft is behind 1st row with 2nd row removed; if 2nd row is kept, max is lower.

notable_other: exclusive sport exterior styling and interior trim
all other EX-L content carried over

changes: 19-inch gloss-black alloy wheels replace 18-inch EX-L wheels
Leather-trimmed seats with red contrast stitching

trims.notes: Mostly an appearance package over EX-L.

notable_other: Rear Entertainment System with 10.2-inch ceiling-mounted display

changes: Rear Entertainment System with 10.2-inch ceiling display added
Bose 500-watt 11-speaker premium audio with subwoofer (vs Touring's 11-speaker non-Bose)

trims.notes: Top Odyssey trim. Note: Edmunds lists Elite at $51,695 — KBB and other sources confirm same.

models.notes: No AWD option offered. No hybrid powertrain offered (unlike Toyota Sienna or Chrysler Pacifica). All trims share 8-passenger seating with Magic Slide 2nd-row, the V6/10-speed powertrain, and standard leather. CabinTalk standard on all; CabinWatch on Touring + Elite only. IIHS has not awarded the Odyssey TSP/TSP+; some IIHS subtests have rated the minivan only Marginal historically, so safety.iihs is null.
